About the Job Role Overview Voice Assistant Interaction Segmentation:  In this role, you will listen to long-form audio recordings between users and Voice Assistance and segment them into distinct, meaningful user interactions Your work ensures that each Voice Assistance-user exchange is accurately identified and contextually complete — helping enhance conversational AI systems and voice recognition models. Labeling & Transcription: You will label speaker turns and accurately transcribe user speech, following detailed annotation guidelines and localized trigger phrases specific to English (Singapore). Your work ensures that each user–Voice Assistant exchange is accurately identified, contextually complete, and linguistically precise, helping improve conversational AI and voice recognition models.  Project Requirements Proficiency in English (Singapore): Must be native in English (Singapore). Listening Skills: Strong comprehension and attention to audio cues such as pauses, tone shifts, and speaker changes. Analytical Thinking: Ability to interpret conversational flow and identify intent boundaries within audio. Work Environment: Remote – work from the comfort of your home. Tools & Access: Must be able to use Centific’s online task platforms and follow provided workflow guidelines.  Key Responsibilities Listen to long audio recordings to identify the start and end of each Voice Assistance-user interaction. Segment the audio into complete, meaningful interactions that include user requests and Voice Assistance’s responses. Group repetitive or follow-up questions into a single segment if they share the same intent. Detect and flag any impostor voices or unclear audio segments. Label speaker turns and accurately transcribe user speech according to provided guidelines Ensure logical and linguistic accuracy for every interaction segment. Maintain high quality and consistency following Centific’s 100% accuracy standard. Participate in feedback or QA sessions to continuously improve performance.  Focus on Model Improvement Your careful segmentation, Labeling & Transcription and attention to conversational detail will directly contribute to improving Voice Assistance’s ability to understand, respond, and learn from natural human speech — making every user interaction smoother and smarter.  Availability Able to commit 4–5 hours per day minimum (full-time preferred). Must adhere to Centific’s working time and productivity expectations.  Communication Strong communication skills and willingness to collaborate with project leads and QA reviewers. Open to participating in training or calibration sessions when required.  Important Note Use of AI tools, automation, or speech recognition software for this task is strictly prohibited. All segmentation must be done manually through active listening. Successful completion of onboarding and certification is mandatory before production. About Centific Centific is a frontier Job Title: 反欺诈调查经理 | Fraud Investigation Manager
Company: HAIER SINGAPORE INVESTMENT HOLDING PTE. LTD.
Job Description: 公司背景 | Company BackgroundHaier 是一家全球化、多元化经营的集团型企业，业务覆盖多个国家和行业，持续推进国际化与规模化发展。随着业务复杂度和跨区域经营不断提升，集团对廉洁合规、风险防控及反舞弊治理提出了更高要求。为进一步强化集团反舞弊体系建设，提升重点领域和平台的风险识别与治理能力，支持业务长期、合规、健康发展，特设立本岗位。Haier is a diversified global enterprise with operations across multiple countries and industries, continuously advancing international expansion and business scale. As business complexity and cross-regional operations increase, the Group has raised higher requirements for integrity, compliance, risk prevention, and anti-fraud governance.To further strengthen the Group’s anti-fraud framework, enhance risk identification and governance capabilities across key domains and platforms, and support long-term, compliant, and healthy business development, this position is established.主要职责 | Key Responsibilities承接集团交办的重点舞弊案件，识别涉案内部人员及外部合作方，推动损失挽回与责任追究，形成震慑效应，支持业务的合规与健康发展。推动反舞弊工作在重点领域和产业的平台化、体系化落地，从业绩差距和长期低效平台切入，系统性解决深层次舞弊与治理问题。搭建并持续优化区域反舞弊管理体系，确保符合当地法律法规及集团制度并有效落地。建立并推广标准化、多语言的舞弊举报渠道，覆盖各业务单元，强化诚信文化与合规意识。负责舞弊案件的闭环管理，持续提升调查质量与效率。以案件为抓手识别流程与内控缺陷，协同业务推动整改方案设计与落地，防止同类风险反复发生。负责反舞弊流程制度体系建设、举报投诉渠道的宣贯，舞弊线索收集，廉洁文化建设及警示教育培训。搭建并优化反舞弊管理与协同机制，加强与总部及集团各部门的沟通协作，提升集团整体反舞弊合力。Take the lead on key fraud cases assigned by Headquarters, identify internal personnel and external partners involved, facilitate loss recovery and accountability, enhance deterrence, and support compliant and sustainable business development.Drive the implementation of anti-fraud efforts across key domains and industries, focusing on performance gaps and long-term underperforming platforms, and addressing entrenched issues through systematic anti-fraud initiatives. Build and continuously optimize the regional anti-fraud management framework in compliance with local laws and Group policies, ensuring effective implementation. Establish and promote standardized, multilingual whistleblowing channels across business units to strengthen integrity culture and compliance awareness.Manage fraud investigations and closed-loop case handling, including effectiveness assessment, to continuously improve investigation quality and efficiency.Leverage fraud cases to identify underlying process and control weaknesses, work with business teams to design and implement corrective actions, and prevent recurrence of similar risks.Be responsible for the establishment of anti-fraud process and system framework, promotion of whistleblowing and complaint channels, collection of fraud-related information, integrity culture building, and implementation of warning education and training programs.Build and optimize anti-fraud governance and collaboration mechanisms, and coordinate closely with relevant departments across the Group to enhance overall anti-fraud effectiveness.岗位要求 | Job Requirements本科及以上学历，法律、审计、会计、财务、风险管理或相关专业优先。具备 8 年以上 反舞弊（包括内部贪腐或受贿）、合规、内部审计或相关法律领域的工作经验，具有跨国或区域经验者优先。具备企业内部员工舞弊、贪腐、受贿等案件的实务调查与处理经验，不限于销售端反商业贿赂，能够独立或主导内部调查工作；优先考虑具备甲方制造业背景者，熟悉制造型企业从采购、研发、生产、销售、物流及供应链管理等全业务流程，对各环节常见舞弊与廉洁风险有系统性认知及实操应对经验；具备将内部调查案件移送司法机关并推进刑事追责的实际经验，熟悉刑民交叉、证据固定及与司法机关沟通流程；具备法律教育或法律相关工作背景者优先（如法学、合规、内控、审计、纪检监察等）。具备独立开展舞弊调查、风险识别与分析的能力。具备制度与流程建设能力，能够推动反舞弊管理体系的落地与执行。具备实际舞弊案件调查及司法处理相关经验。具备良好的组织能力、沟通协调能力及团队协作精神，具备问题识别与前瞻性判断能力，积极主动，愿意接受工作挑战。英语及普通话可作为工作语言，具备良好的跨文化、跨部门沟通及汇报能力。持有 CFE 等相关专业证书者优先。可接受必要的全球出差。Bachelor’s degree or above, preferably in Law, Auditing, Accounting, Finance, Risk Management, or related fields.At least 8 years of experience in anti-fraud (including internal corruption or bribery), compliance, internal audit, or related legal roles, with multinational or regional experience preferred.Proven experience handling internal employee corruption/bribery cases within enterprises; experience limited only to sales-side anti-commercial bribery is not sufficient.Preferably has a background on the owner/client side in the manufacturing industry, with understanding of fraud and corruption risks and hands-on handling experience across the full business process, including procurement, R&D, sales, logistics, and the supply chain.Practical experience in transferring cases to judicial authorities for criminal prosecution; a legal background is preferred.Strong ability to independently conduct fraud investigations, risk identification, and analysis.Proven capability in designing and implementing anti-fraud policies, systems, and processes.Hands-on experience in fraud case investigations and related legal or judicial proceedings.Strong organizational, communication, coordination, and team collaboration skills, with the ability to identify issues and exercise forward-looking judgment, and a proactive mindset with willingness to take on work challenges.Fluent in English and Mandarin as working languages, with strong cross-cultural and cross-functional communication and reporting skills.Relevant professional certifications, such as Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE), are preferred.Willing to travel globally as required.

ABOUT FRASERS PROPERTY LIMITEDFrasers Property Limited (“Frasers Property” and together with its subsidiaries, the “Frasers Property Group” or the “Group”), is a multinational investor-developer-manager of real estate products and services. The Group is listed on the Main Board of the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading Limited (“SGX-ST”) and headquartered in Singapore. Frasers Property's multinational businesses operate across five asset classes, namely, commercial & business parks, hospitality, industrial & logistics, residential and retail. The Group has businesses in Southeast Asia, Australia, the EU, the UK and China, and its well-established hospitality business owns and/or operates serviced apartments and hotels in over 20 countries across Asia, Australia, Europe, the Middle East and Africa.   Frasers Property is also the sponsor of two real estate investment trusts (“REITs”) and one stapled trust listed on the SGX-ST. Frasers Centrepoint Trust and Frasers Logistics & Commercial Trust are focused on retail, and industrial & commercial properties, respectively. Frasers Hospitality Trust (comprising Frasers Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and Frasers Hospitality Business Trust) is a stapled trust focused on hospitality properties. In addition, the Group has two REITs listed on the Stock Exchange of Thailand. Frasers Property (Thailand) Public Company Limited is the sponsor of Frasers Property Thailand Industrial Freehold & Leasehold REIT, which is focused on industrial & logistics properties in Thailand, and Golden Ventures Leasehold Real Estate Investment Trust, which is focused on commercial properties.   The Group is committed to inspiring experiences and creating places for good for its stakeholders. By acting progressively, producing and consuming responsibly, and focusing on its people, Frasers Property aspires to raise sustainability ideals across its value chain, and build a more resilient business. It is committed to be a net-zero carbon corporation by 2050. Building on its heritage as well as leveraging its knowledge and capabilities, the Group aims to create lasting shared value for its people, the businesses and communities it serves. We are seeking fluent English (Singapore) speakers to join a large-scale audio annotation project on a freelance / gig basis.No technical background is required, this is a great opportunity to earn additional income while working from home.In this role, you will listen to recorded conversations between a User and a Voice Assistant (e.g., Siri or an assistant impersonator) and apply precise, time-stamped labels according to clear, client-provided guidelines. Tasks include identifying speaker turns, marking when each person speaks, distinguishing between the User, Assistant, and (in some recordings) a third “Bystander” speaker, and segmenting conversational events accurately.Approximately 45% of recordings include a bystander who introduces natural distractions such as side comments or background speech. Recordings may contain varying levels of environmental noise (e.g., quiet indoor settings, appliance sounds, TV audio, or music). You will follow detailed instructions, complete practice tasks within the client’s annotation tool, perform self-checks to ensure accuracy, and participate in quality review processes when required. The work is repetitive but straightforward and highly quality-focused.This is a fully remote, project-based opportunity with a minimum commitment aligned to project timelines.Work must be completed manually through active listening. The use of AI tools, automation, or speech recognition software is strictly prohibited.Project PurposeThis project supports the development and evaluation of advanced speech recognition systems by accurately labeling conversational audio events. Your work will help improve the accuracy and performance of next-generation voice recognition technology.Why Join Us?100% remote work (work from home)Flexible freelance engagementOpportunity to contribute directly to real-world AI improvementInternational and collaborative project environmentPotential for ongoing, project-based collaborationRequirementsNative-level fluency in English (Singapore) with strong familiarity with local speech patterns and accentsStrong listening comprehension and ability to distinguish multiple speakersExcellent attention to detail and consistencyAbility to follow structured linguistic and technical guidelinesSuccessful completion of OneForma Certification and KYC verificationSuccessful completion of required practice tasks before production workReliable computer and stable internet connectionAvailability to meet assigned deadlines and project timelinesComfortable working independently in a remote environmentPreferred SkillsBasic computer proficiencyAnalytical mindset with strong focus on qualityExperience in transcription, annotation, linguistics, or data labeling is a plus (not required)Ability to work with audio that includes background noise or overlapping speechPayment & Engagement TypeFreelance / project-based engagementPayments processed via OneForma’s monthly billing cycle and subject to client approvalImportant InformationWork must be completed manually; use of AI tools or speech recognition software is strictly prohibitedSuccessful completion of onboarding, certification, and tool practice tasks is mandatory before starting productionAccuracy, consistency, and strict adherence to guidelines are essentialQuality performance will be closely monitoredDetailed instructions and training materials will be providedThis role contributes directly to improving real-world conversational AI systems

STATEMENT OF PURPOSEYou will be part of the Group Digital Health team, responsible for the day-to-day operational management of a self-service kiosk application deployed across NHG Health institutions. 
The role ensures stable operations, effective issue triage, coordinated releases, and strong collaboration across clinical, operational, technology, and corporate stakeholders, leveraging both operational and technical understanding in a complex public healthcare environment.
MAJOR DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Operational Management 

Oversee day-to-day operations of the self-service kiosk, ensuring service stability and timely resolution of issues
Manage and oversee Level 1 (L1) Support Helpdesk, including escalation, prioritisation, and follow-through
Own incident, issue, and defect triage, performing initial technical assessment and working closely with the product, engineering, vendors and institution ops teams
Ensure issues are clearly documented, logged, tracked, and resolved according to agreed SLAs with sufficient technical detail to support root cause analysis
Perform initial technical triage using available logs, dashboards, and monitoring tools and coordinate escalation to relevant technical teams
Act as the operational lead during incidents, coordinating response activities, communications, and escalation until resolution 

Release Management

Coordinate application releases, including release communications, operational readiness, and post-release monitoring, and technical issue follow-up
Work with internal teams to ensure minimal disruption to hospital operations during changes or updates
Support institutional go-live and onboarding readiness, including coordination of operational checklists, support readiness, and post-go-live stabilisation

Stakeholder Management

Maintain regular communication with internal and external stakeholders to ensure alignment on operations and priorities
Partner with cross-functional teams (product, IT, engineering, clinical ops, vendors) and manage vendor operational performance
Coordinate with Legal, CIO, and CISO offices to ensure operational compliance and risk management

Continuous Improvement

Track and report operational metrics (e.g. response times, resolution turnaround, recurring issues)
Identify opportunities to improve operational processes, efficiency, and support capacity
Ensure operational activities remain on time and within budget

Knowledge Management

Oversee development and maintenance of staff user guides and operational SOPs and technical support documentation where required
Identify training needs and coordinate training for support staff where required

OTHER DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Provide operational, digital, or technical input to other Group Digital Health initiatives or projects on an as-needed basis, where relevant experience and perspectives can add value to solution design, service readiness, or delivery planning.
Participate in cross-project forums, reviews, or working groups to share insights and best practices drawn from live system operations and prior digital or technical experience.
Perform other duties as assigned that are aligned with the role’s core responsibilities and expertise.

·       Support electronic and manual data collection, including programming surveys and assessments into digital platforms.
·       Conduct fieldwork, such as administering surveys, conducting interviews, and facilitating focus groups with community members.
·       Ensure data quality through monitoring, cleaning, and preparing datasets for analysis using statistical software (e.g., Stata, R).

·       Assist with preparing materials for data collection and training sessions for field staff.
·       Support the preparation and submission of Institutional Review Board (IRB) applications and ensure compliance with ethical standards.

·       Conduct basic quantitative and qualitative data analyses, including descriptive statistics and coding of transcripts.
·       Collaborate with research staff to produce data visualizations and initial findings for reports and presentations.

·       Coordinate logistics for fieldwork, including scheduling, resource allocation, and troubleshooting challenges during data collection.
·       Support the research team in administrative tasks, such as managing files, tracking progress, and assisting with grant or contract documentation.
6. Adherence to Research Integrity and Confidentiality
·       Disclose any conflicts of interest and ensure all research activities are conducted with integrity and transparency.
·       Safeguard participant data and maintain confidentiality throughout the research process.
